operator_scope.semantic_signature: irregular indexed memory access after correctness passes

valid_when: Task uses gather rows, embedding lookup, or scatter-add with an explicit index tensor and a correct candidate exists.
Profile summary indicates irregular/uncoalesced memory or atomic contention.
invalid_when: The access pattern is a regular transpose/permute copy with affine strides only.
Correctness for bounds, index dtype, and duplicate scatter indices has not passed.
preconditions: Apply sem_layout_stride_indexing_v1 and sem_scatter_add_collision_v1 first when applicable.

mechanism: Irregular indices break the coalescing assumptions of regular copies. The implementation must separately reason about index loads, source row reads, destination writes, and scatter collisions; performance depends strongly on index distribution.

instructions: Separate the schedule into index load, source/gather read or scatter destination calculation, and output write; inspect which leg is contiguous and coalesce that leg first.
For gather_rows or embedding_lookup, tile within each row so lanes copy contiguous columns when the row payload is contiguous; do not assume adjacent row indices are adjacent in memory.
For scatter_add, choose atomics for general duplicates or a staged per-block reduction only when the task and index distribution justify it.
Keep bounds checks on index values even in performance candidates; mask or reject out-of-range indices according to OpSpec.
Benchmark with both low-collision and high-collision index distributions before promoting any scatter-add performance rule.

expected_effect.correctness: No effect if semantic capsules are followed; can reveal missing bounds/collision checks during tuning.
expected_effect.performance: Candidate-only: may improve coalescing for row-wise gathers or reduce wasted scatter attempts; no stable H20 claim.
expected_effect.search_efficiency: Prevents applying regular transpose tiling blindly to irregular memory tasks.
expected_effect.risk: Index distribution can dominate performance; a schedule tuned on one visible distribution may fail held-out distributions.

correctness_matrix: row payload widths not divisible by tile
performance_requirements: Report index distribution assumptions with benchmark results.
Do not promote from a single collision pattern.
validation.promotion_policy: Requires H20 measurements across at least low-collision and high-collision cases plus profile attribution.

known_failures: Assuming gather indices are sorted or contiguous can overfit public inputs.
Plain stores in scatter-add lose updates under collisions.
Removing bounds checks for speed can create illegal memory accesses.

replace_on_new_backend: atomic dtype support, cache behavior, coalescing granularity
